titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to The Church of the Redeemer (New Haven, Conn.) Records - Addendum A

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday November 27, 2024 at 18:12

did.unittitle: Church of the Redeemer (New Haven, Conn.) Records - Addendum A
did.abstract: Reports and publications document the history of The Church of the Redeemer, United Church of Christ (New Haven, Conn.)

corpname.part: Church of the Redeemer (New Haven, Conn.)
Chapel Street Congregational Church (New Haven, Conn.)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Historical statement by Elliott Barske, Church Historian

In 1838 the City of New Haven celebrated its bicentennial year and the fourth Congregational Church in the area came into being when on November 4th the Chapel Street Congregational Church was organized and declared a Church of Christ with 61 members. This congregation was eventually to become the Church of the Redeemer.

Two hundred years before in 1638, the New Haven Colony was settled under the leadership of Reverend John Davenport and Theophilus Eaton who established the First Church, now Center Church on the Green. Gradually over the early years schisms arose and the period of The Great Awakening and the teachings of Jonathan Edwards led to the formation of dissenting groups such as the White Haven Church and the Fair Haven Society. These two groups merged in 1796 to form North Church on the Green, the Second Church of New Haven, now known as United Church.

The Third Congregational Church was formed in 1826 under the leadership of Dr. Nathaniel W. Taylor, a Professor of Theology in Yale College and a former pastor of Center Church. The founding members of the Third included 19 from Center Church and 10 from United Church. This congregation was disrupted after a new pastor, Dr. E. L. Cleaveland, who was installed in 1833, would not accept the "new theology" of Nathaniel Taylor. Many members of Third stood by Dr. Taylor and under his spiritual guidance organized and founded the Chapel Street Congregational Church. At the organization meeting Timothy Dwight, Jr., son of the President of Yale, acted as Chairman and became very active in the government of the new church.

During the over 170 years of our history, there have been 17 Pastors of whom the longest serving were Reverend John E. Todd 1869-1890 and Reverend Watson L. Phillips 1890-1915. Both were serving during the two major relocations of the church. Todd was present when our Second House of Worship at Orange and Wall Streets was dedicated on May 25, 1871 and the name changed to the Church of the Redeemer. Phillips was involved in the move to Cold Spring Street and Whitney Ave where the Parish House was dedicated on October 10, 1920. After 30 years of planning and fund raising the present Main Sanctuary was completed and dedicated on May 20, 1951 during the pastorate of the Reverend Robert E. Luccock.

MINISTRIES PAST TO PRESENT Rev. John O. Colton 1839-1840 Rev. Joseph P. Thompson 1840-1845 Rev. Leverett Griggs 1845-1847 Rev. William T. Eustis 1848-1869 Rev. John E. Todd 1869-1890 Rev. Watson L. Phillips 1890-1915 Rev. Roy M. Houghton 1917-1931 Rev. R. Hawley Fitch 1932-1935 Rev. Phillip G. Scott 1936-1949 Rev. Robert E. Luccock 1950-1962 Rev. Charles L. Ives 1962-1964 Rev. C. Arthur Bradley 1964-1981 Rev. Dr. Thomas Burgett-Leutner 1981-1994 Rev. Olivia Robinson 1994-1996 (interim) Rev. Lillian F. Daniel 1996-2004 Rev. Dr. Duncan Newcomer 2004-2006 (interim) Rev. Dr. Rochelle A. Stackhouse 2006-Present

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports and publications document the history of The Church of the Redeemer, United Church of Christ (New Haven, Conn.)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Church of the Redeemer (New Haven, Conn.) Records - Addendum A (RG 266A), Special Collections, Yale Divinity Library.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Early records of the church, likely duplicated at the New Haven Museum.

did.unittitle: Chapel Street Congregational Church (Chapel St and Union Ave)
Church of the Redeemer (Orange and Wall Sts.)
Church of the Redeemer (Orange and Wall Sts.)
Church of the Redeemer (Orange and Wall Sts.)
Church of the Redeemer (Whitney Ave at Cold Spring St., as of 1920)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Series 3, Church Governing Bodies, includes records of the Church Council, Board of Deacons, Christian Education and various church committees.

did.unittitle: Executive Committee of the Church Council
Job descriptions of Christian Education staff
Minutes, including Our Christian World Missions/Our Church's Wider Mission
Clerk Files: Membership and Staff Records
The Rev. C. Arthur Bradley: sermons, prayers
Installation of the Rev. Thomas A. Burgett-Leutner (a.k.a. Thomas A. Leutner), Senior Minister
Ordination of Tina F. Burgett-Leutner (a.k.a. Tina F. Burgett)
Installation of Elizabeth C. Myrick, Associate Minister
Service of celebration and recognition of the ministry of The Rev. Ron Kittel
The Rev. Lillian F. Daniel, Senior Minister: sermons
The Rev. Dr. Richard Wood: sermon
The Rev. William Oliver III: sermons
Ordination of Samuel Adam Dexter, Associate Minister
The Rev. Dr. Duncan D. Newcomer, Interim Senior Minister: sermons
Installation of The Rev. Dr. Rochelle A. Stackhouse, Senior Minister
Bulletins, 2016 Jan-Jun (incl. 2016 Confirmation class faith statements)
Bulletins, 2017 Jan-Jun (incl. Godspeed service for Rev. Shelly Stackhouse, May 7)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Godspeed Service for The Rev. Rochelle A. Stackhouse on May 7, 2017.

did.unittitle: Bulletins, 2017 Jul-Dec (incl. Rev. Susan J. Murtha as Transitional Minister, beginning Oct 15)

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Rev. Susan J. Murtha began as Interim Senior Minister/Transitional Minister on October 15, 2017.

did.unittitle: Bulletin, 2019 Apr 28 (Final Service of Thanksgiving and Celebration)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Final Service of Thanksgiving and Celebration

did.unittitle: Communion tankards (2) and chalices (2), silver plate
did.materialspec: Rogers, Smith and Company, Gold and Silver Plate

did.unittitle: Awards won by or awarded to The Church of the Redeemer and/or Welcome Hall, 1923-1938
Junior League Defenders Cup, Interchurch Basketball, YMCA
Interchurch Basketball, Intermediate Leauge, won by Welcome Hall
Interchurch Baseball Junior League, won by Welcome Hall
Interchurch Baseball Junior League, won by Welcome Hall
Interchurch Basketball Intermediate League, won by Church of the Redeemer
Interchurch Basketball Junior League, won by Welcome Hall
Interchurch Basketball Intermediate League, won by Welcome Hall
Church Reg Fed New Haven YMCA Baseball, won by Welcome Hall
Junior's 2nd Division, T. A. League, won by Church of the Redeemer Presented by Tamber E. Loas
Kirby Defenders Trophy, Intermediate Basketball League of the Interchurch Boys Federation. Winners listed.
Kirby Trophy, Annual Stunt Night, Interchurch Older Boys Federation
Plaques in memory of church benefactors
did.materialspec: Wood plaques with gold engraved plates describing objects and their provenance. Most were gifts from church members, groups, or ministers.
